title: Search Autocomplete
prompt: Design a search autocomplete system like Google's search suggestions. As the user types a query, the system should suggest the most relevant completions in real-time, based on popular searches and optionally personalized to the user.

functional: Return top-k suggestions as the user types each character
Support personalized suggestions based on user search history
Update suggestion rankings as search trends change
Filter out inappropriate or offensive suggestions
nonFunctional: Suggestions should reflect recent trends within minutes
Graceful degradation: serve stale suggestions if real-time data is unavailable

scale.qps: 60K searches/sec, ~300K autocomplete requests/sec (5 keystrokes per search avg)

keyQuestions: How many suggestions should we return per keystroke (top 5? top 10?)
Do we need personalized suggestions or only popularity-based?
How fresh should the trending data be (real-time vs hourly)?
Do we need to support spelling correction or fuzzy matching?
How do we handle offensive or inappropriate suggestions?
What languages need to be supported?

keyCalculations: Trie size: 10B unique prefixes * 50 bytes avg = 500 GB
Per-node memory: with sharding across 100 nodes = 5 GB per node (fits in RAM)
Search log volume: 60K searches/sec * 100 bytes = 6 MB/sec = 500 GB/day
Cache hit ratio: top 10K prefixes cover 80% of queries => high cache effectiveness

expectedEndpoints: POST /api/v1/search/log { query, userId, timestamp } => (async search logging)

expectedComponents: Trie Builder (offline MapReduce job to rebuild trie from search logs)
Aggregation Service (count queries per time window, apply decay)
Personalization Service (blend user history with global popularity)
Content Filter (blocklist check before returning results)
Zookeeper / Config Service (shard mapping, feature flags)
CDN / Edge Cache (cache popular prefixes at edge)

suggestedTopics: Trie data structure: compressed trie vs ternary search tree, memory optimization
Ranking algorithm: frequency decay (exponential time-weighted scoring) for trending queries
Sharding strategy: prefix-based sharding vs hash-based, handling hot prefixes
Real-time updates: streaming pipeline (Kafka + Flink) to update trie without full rebuild
Personalization: blending global popularity with user history (alpha * global + (1-alpha) * personal)
Edge caching: caching top prefixes at CDN edge for sub-10ms latency

checklist: Asked about number of suggestions to return
Clarified personalization requirements
Discussed freshness of trending data
Asked about content moderation needs
Estimated scale (QPS per keystroke, not just per search)

anchors.1: Skipped requirements, immediately started drawing a trie
anchors.2: Asked 1-2 questions but missed latency requirements or personalization scope
anchors.3: Covered latency, scale, personalization, and content moderation requirements
anchors.4: Proactively identified per-keystroke QPS amplification, trending freshness needs, and multi-language complexity

checklist: Used trie or similar prefix-optimized data structure
Separated online serving from offline trie building
Included data collection pipeline for search logs
Addressed caching at multiple levels
Included content moderation in the serving path

anchors.1: Database LIKE query for every keystroke with no caching
anchors.2: Trie-based lookup but monolithic system with no pipeline for updates
anchors.3: In-memory trie serving, offline build pipeline, caching layer, and log collection
anchors.4: Multi-layer architecture with edge caching, streaming updates, personalization blending, and graceful degradation

checklist: Explained trie structure and memory optimization
Discussed ranking/scoring algorithm with time decay
Addressed trie update strategy (rebuild vs streaming)
Considered sharding and hot prefix handling
Discussed personalization implementation

anchors.1: No depth on trie implementation or ranking algorithm
anchors.2: Basic trie explanation but no discussion of updates, sharding, or ranking
anchors.3: Deep dive into trie optimization + ranking algorithm or update strategy with trade-offs
anchors.4: Thorough analysis covering compressed tries, streaming updates, frequency decay math, and sharding with hot-prefix mitigation

checklist: Trie vs inverted index for prefix lookups
Full trie rebuild vs incremental streaming updates
Memory vs disk for trie storage
Global vs personalized ranking
Freshness vs consistency of suggestions

anchors.3: Discussed 2-3 trade-offs with pros/cons and justified choices
anchors.4: Systematically evaluated data structure, update strategy, and caching trade-offs with latency and cost analysis

commonMistakes: Using database LIKE queries instead of a trie, leading to unacceptable latency
Not accounting for per-keystroke QPS (5-10x amplification over search QPS)
Rebuilding the entire trie on every search instead of batched/streaming updates
Ignoring content moderation, allowing offensive suggestions to surface
Not considering hot prefixes (e.g., 'a', 'th') that concentrate load on specific shards
Treating personalization as a simple filter instead of a blended ranking signal
